semantic.role: SVG architecture diagram renderer that transforms a knowledge graph into an SVG representation using a layered (Sugiyama-style) layout engine.
responsibilities: Converts a KnowledgeGraph (nodes and edges) into an SVG string suitable for architecture diagram visualization.
Implements a topological-sort based layered layout algorithm (Sugiyama-equivalent) to assign y-coordinates to layers and position nodes within layers.
Generates SVG elements for nodes (rectangles with labels) and edges (polylines with arrowheads), handling XML escaping and basic styling.
Provides the single exported function `generateArchitectureDiagramSvg` as the public API for downstream view services.
outOfScope: Does not produce architecture diagrams in non-SVG formats (e.g., ASCII, Mermaid).
Does not handle interactive features like click events or zoom controls.
Does not perform semantic analysis or filtering of the knowledge graph; it only renders whatever graph is passed.
Does not read or write files directly; SVG output is returned as a string.
invariants: Input must be a valid KnowledgeGraph object with nodes and edges arrays.
Output SVG must be a well-formed XML string that can be embedded in HTML or saved as .svg file.
Node positions are computed deterministically based on graph topology; the same graph always produces the same layout.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a standalone, deterministic SVG renderer for architecture diagrams that can be used by view rendering pipelines without external layout dependencies (e.g., dagre).
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Part of ongoing refactor to remove legacy CLI aliases and rebuild documentation; no direct changes to layout algorithm or public API expected from this commit.

publicSurface.description: Takes a KnowledgeGraph and returns an SVG string representing a layered architecture diagram. This is the sole public API consumed by the view service layer to produce diagram views.
